name: Sea Turtle
summary: Green sea turtles are large ocean reptiles with paddle-shaped flippers and hard shells. They graze on seagrass, migrate across warm seas, and hatch from sandy beaches.
metaTitle: Sea Turtle Facts | Ocean Habitat, Diet & Photos
metaDescription: Discover green sea turtle facts with photos, reef habitat, seagrass diet, hatchling life, and conservation. Learn where sea turtles live and why they are endangered.
searchIntents: how long do sea turtles live

lifespan.captivity: Up to 80 years in human care

speed: Up to 20 mph swimming in short bursts

reproduction.gestationOrIncubation: Eggs incubate in sand for about 45 to 70 days

funFacts: Green sea turtles are named for the green color of their body fat, not their shell.
Females often return to the same beach where they hatched to lay eggs.
Sea turtles breathe air but can stay underwater for hours while resting.
Hatchlings are tiny but must reach the ocean quickly to survive.
A sea turtle shell is called a carapace on top and a plastron underneath.
Green sea turtles can migrate across entire ocean basins.

faq.question: What is a sea turtle?
faq.answer: A sea turtle is a reptile that lives in the ocean. This page focuses on the green sea turtle, which grazes on seagrass and algae.

faq.question: Where do sea turtles live?
faq.answer: Green sea turtles live in warm coastal oceans around the world, including reefs, bays, and seagrass meadows.

faq.question: What do sea turtles eat?
faq.answer: Adult green sea turtles mainly eat seagrass and algae. Young turtles may eat more small animals early in life.

faq.question: Are sea turtles endangered?
faq.answer: Green sea turtles are listed as Endangered because of fishing nets, plastic pollution, boat strikes, and habitat loss.

faq.question: How do sea turtles breathe?
faq.answer: Sea turtles breathe air with lungs and must surface to breathe, though they can stay underwater for long periods while resting.

faq.question: Why do sea turtles come onto beaches?
faq.answer: Females come ashore to dig nests and lay eggs in the sand. Hatchlings then crawl to the ocean after hatching.

faq.question: How long do sea turtles live?
faq.answer: Green sea turtles can live for several decades, and some may survive more than 70 years.
